title: Leptilon pusillum
intro: Leptilon pusillum, also known as Small Purple Evening Primrose, is a small, annual herbaceous plant belonging to the Onagraceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and as an expectorant to relieve coughs and congestion. The bioactive constituents of Leptilon pusillum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Leptilon pusillum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are utilized to alleviate colds, bronchitis, and other respiratory ailments.
taxonomy: Leptilon pusillum is a small annual herb that belongs to the family Asteraceae. Its classification also includes the tribe Heliantheae and the subtribe Eutaxiinae. This plant is native to North America and is often found in fields and along roadsides. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Leptilon pusillum shares characteristics with other flowering plants in this group. The specific classification of Leptilon pusillum may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted as a member of the family Asteraceae.
